chapter_title.ar: باب وَقْتِ الصَّلَاةِ فِي يَوْمِ الْغَيْمِ وَ الرِّيحِ وَ مَنْ صَلَّى لِغَيْرِ الْقِبْلَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 8 – The time for Salāt during a cloudy and windy day, and the one who prays Salāt to other than the Qiblah

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/50)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/50)
text: إِذَا صَلَّيْتَ وَ أَنْتَ تَرَى أَنَّكَ فِي وَقْتٍ وَ لَمْ يَدْخُلِ الْوَقْتُ فَدَخَلَ الْوَقْتُ وَ أَنْتَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ فَقَدْ أَجْزَأَتْ عَنْكَ.

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Ibn Abu Umer, from Ismail Bin Rabaah,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘When you are praying Salāt and you see that you are within the time, and the time has not entered yet, then the time does enter while you are during the Salāt, so it would have sufficed you’.
en.sarwar: 11. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from ibn abu ‘Umayr from ’Isma‘il ibn Rabah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If you perform Salah (prayer) and you think it is time, when in fact is not time yet, but the time comes when you are still performing that Salah (prayer) it is sufficient.’”
